---
title: "<img src='ARC_Logo.png' style='float: right; width: 250px;'/>**Platelet:RBC Ratio by LTOWB Usage 2018-2025** "
author: "American Red Cross Biomedical Services"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%a %d %B %Y')`"
# output: html_document
always_allow_html: true
output:
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
    df_print: paged
    # css: style.css
    includes:
      # in_header: "/ARC_Logo.png"
  classoption: landscape
    # mainfont: Arial
  pdf_document: 
    keep_tex: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
  word_document: default
  # github_document: default
  fig_caption: yes
  keep_md: yes

---

## Temporal Patterns in Platelet‑to‑Red Cell Transfusion Ratios Across Hospitals With Varying LTOWB Utilization{.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}
### Median PLT:RBC by LTWOB Usage
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)       # mixed models
library(lmerTest)   # p-values & Type III tests (Satterthwaite/KR)
library(emmeans)    # marginal means & pairwise comparisons
set.seed(123)


df <- read_excel('E:\\ARC\\Projects\\LTOWB PLT RBC\\ltowb\\ltwobhosp.xlsx')
df <- df %>% filter(!is.na(customer_cd), !is.na(pltrbcratio)) 
# Ensure factor ordering (optional)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    ltwobcat = factor(ltwobcat, levels = c("<2.5","2.5-5.0",">5")),
    calendar_yr = as.integer(calendar_yr)
  )

# Summarize: mean and SE (cluster-robust CIs optional in B)
summary_by_year <- df %>%
  group_by(ltwobcat, calendar_yr) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_pltrbc = median(pltrbcratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_pltrbc = sd(pltrbcratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_pltrbc = sd_pltrbc / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ci_l = median_pltrbc - qt(0.975, pmax(n-1,1)) * se_pltrbc,
    ci_u = median_pltrbc + qt(0.975, pmax(n-1,1)) * se_pltrbc
  )

# Line with points and ribbons by category
ggplot(summary_by_year,
       aes(x = calendar_yr, y = median_pltrbc, color = ltwobcat, group = ltwobcat)) +
  # geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_l, ymax = ci_u, fill = ltwobcat),
  #             color = NA, alpha = 0.12, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(summary_by_year$calendar_yr))) +
  labs(
    x = "Calendar year",
    y = "PLT:RBC ratio",
    color = "LTWOB %usage",
    title = "Change in PLT:RBC ratio by LTWOB category over time (descriptive medians)"
  ) +
  theme_pubclean(base_size = 12) +
  ylim(0,1.2)
```

- Across eight years of observation, only hospitals with >5% LTWOB usage demonstrated a substantial temporary rise in PLT:RBC ratios, peaking in 2020, with subsequent decline and convergence toward the lower utilization categories by 2023–2025.
- These descriptive patterns suggest early implementation‑related variability in high‑LTWOB hospitals, with stabilization of transfusion ratios over time.
  + *Note: These values represent descriptive medians and do not reflect model‑adjusted estimates or tests of statistical significance.*


### Comparing hosp. by LTOWB% for PLT:RBC by year{.tabset}
#### Mixed model: Random intercept for hospital
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
library(pbkrtest)
df -> hosp
hosp <- hosp %>%
  mutate(
    year_f = factor(calendar_yr),              # like calendar_yr_c in SAS
    customer_cd = factor(customer_cd)
  )

#Fit linear mixed model (Gaussian, identity link; REML ~ PROC MIXED default)
m <- lmer(pltrbcratio ~ ltwobcat * year_f + (1 | customer_cd), data = hosp, REML = TRUE)

#--- Global Type III tests (focus on the interaction)
a <- anova(m, type = 3, ddf = "Kenward-Roger") #If pbkrtest is not installed, lmerTest will fall back to Satterthwaite

# Replace row names with publication‑ready labels
rownames(a) <- c("LTWOB category",
                 "Calendar year",
                 "LTWOB category × year")

a
```

- A linear mixed model (hospital random intercept) showed a highly significant 
**LTWOB category × year** interaction (F(12, 410.8) = 19.5, p < 2.2×10⁻¹⁶), 
  + indicating that differences in PLT:RBC ratio across LTWOB adoption levels 
    changed substantially over time.
- Main effects for **year** (p < 10⁻⁹) and **LTWOB** category (p = 0.019) were also significant, 
but are not interpreted because of the strong interaction.

#### Within-year tests
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
# ======================================================
# 4) Within-year tests: Do LTWOB categories differ *in each year*?
#    Tukey-adjusted pairwise differences and overall F per year
# ======================================================
emm <- emmeans(m, ~ ltwobcat | year_f)   # LS-means by category within each year
# Tukey-adjusted pairwise comparisons within each year
pw <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey")

# Convert to clean data frame
pw_df <- as.data.frame(pw)

library(knitr)
remotes::install_github("haozhu233/kableExtra")
library(kableExtra)
# Clean and format the output
pw_clean <- pw_df %>%
  mutate(
    Year     = as.character(year_f),
    Contrast = contrast,
    Estimate = ifelse(is.na(estimate), "nonEst", sprintf("%.3f", estimate)),
    SE       = ifelse(is.na(SE),       "NA",     sprintf("%.3f", SE)),
    DF       = ifelse(is.na(df),       "NA",     sprintf("%.0f", df)),
    t_ratio  = ifelse(is.na(t.ratio),  "NA",     sprintf("%.2f", t.ratio)),
    p_value  = ifelse(
                  is.na(p.value), 
                  "NA",
                  ifelse(p.value < 0.0001,
                         "<0.0001",
                         sprintf("%.4f", p.value)))
  ) %>%
  select(Year, Contrast, Estimate, SE, DF, t_ratio, p_value)

# Render as formatted publication-style table
pw_clean %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Tukey-adjusted pairwise differences in PLT:RBC ratio by LTWOB category within each calendar year.",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = "lcccccc"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")

```

- The output shows within‑year comparisons among the 3 LTWOB categories:
  + '<2.5% LTOWB use'
  + '2.5–5.0% LTOWB use'
  + '>5% LTOWB use'
 
- These are LS‑mean (model‑adjusted) differences in PLT:RBC ratio, computed separately for each year.
  + The Tukey method adjusts for the 3 comparisons within each year.
  
- **2018**: No estimable comparisons.
- **2019**: No differences.
- **2020**: highly significant spike in the >5 category vs both other groups.
  + <2.5 and 2.5–5.0 do not differ (p ≈ 0.99).
  + Both are dramatically lower than >5 (difference ≈ −3.0 units, p < 0.0001).
  + **Conclusion (2020)**: The hospitals with >5% LTOWB usage have significantly higher PLT:RBC than both other LTWOB groups.
- **2021**: No differences.
- **2022**: *Reverse effect*: >5 category now has significantly lower PLT:RBC ratio than both 2.5–5.0 and <2.5.
<2.5 and 2.5–5.0 do not differ significantly. Both have significantly higher PLT:RBC ratio than the >5 category.
**Conclusion:** A reversal compared to 2020. Now hospitals with >5% LTOWB usage have significantly lower PLT:RBC ratio.
- **2023–2025**: No meaningful differences; groups converge in 2023; LTWOB categories remain similar 

### Model-adjusted means 
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
emm_df <- as.data.frame(emm) %>%
  rename(emmean = emmean, lower = lower.CL, upper = upper.CL) %>%
  mutate(calendar_yr = as.integer(as.character(year_f)))

ggplot(emm_df, aes(calendar_yr, emmean, color = ltwobcat, group = ltwobcat)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("<2.5"="#e15759","2.5-5.0"="#59a14f",">5"="#4e79a7"),
                     name = "LTWOB category") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(emm_df$calendar_yr))) +
  labs(
    title = "Adjusted PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals categorized by LTWOB % over time (mixed model LS-means)",
    x = "Calendar year", y = "Adjusted PLT:RBC ratio (LS-mean)"
  ) +
  theme_pubclean(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

### Effect sizes{.tabset}
#### Effect sizes as differences vs a reference category by year{.tabset}
##### ">5 minus <2.5"
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
ref_contr <- contrast(emm, method = list(">5 minus <2.5" = c(-1, 0, 1)), adjust = "none")
res <- summary(ref_contr, infer = TRUE)

res_clean <- as.data.frame(res) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Year = year_f,
    Contrast = contrast,
    Estimate = estimate,
    Lower.CL = lower.CL,
    Upper.CL = upper.CL,
    P.value = p.value
  )

res_clean <- res_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Estimate = ifelse(is.na(Estimate), "nonEst", sprintf("%.3f", Estimate)),
    Lower.CL = ifelse(is.na(Lower.CL), "NA", sprintf("%.3f", Lower.CL)),
    Upper.CL = ifelse(is.na(Upper.CL), "NA", sprintf("%.3f", Upper.CL)),
    P.value  = ifelse(
                 is.na(P.value), "NA",
                 ifelse(P.value < 0.0001, "<0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", P.value))
               )
  )

res_clean %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Adjusted Difference (>5% minus 2.5–5%) in PLT:RBC Ratio by Year",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = "lcccccc"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```


- These estimates represent adjusted mean differences in PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals with >5% LTWOB 
usage and those with <2.5%, generated from a linear mixed model including a hospital‑level random intercept 
and a LTWOB category × year interaction. 
  + Positive values indicate higher PLT:RBC ratios in the >5% group; 
  + Negative values indicate lower ratios. 
  + Confidence intervals are 95%, with Kenward–Roger degrees of freedom.
  + The adjusted difference in PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals with >5% versus <2.5% 
  + LTWOB utilization varied substantially over time. 

- A pronounced elevation in the >5% group was observed in 2020 (difference, 3.03; p < 0.0001), representing a strong 
early divergence in component use.
- By 2022, this pattern reversed, with the >5% group demonstrating significantly lower adjusted ratios 
(difference, −0.90; p = 0.012).
- No significant differences were observed in 2019 or 2021, nor in 2023–2025, indicating a return to alignment 
across LTWOB categories in later years.
- The adjusted difference in PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals with >5% and <2.5 LTWOB utilization varied 
substantially over time (significant interaction). A marked elevation in PLT:RBC ratio was observed 
among >5% hospitals in 2020, with adjusted differences exceeding 3 units compared with the <2.5% group (p < 0.0001).
- By 2022, this pattern reversed, with the >5% group demonstrating significantly lower adjusted ratios 
(difference ≈ −0.9; p = 0.01).
- For all subsequent years (2023–2025), the categories showed no meaningful differences, with point estimates clustered near zero.

##### ">5 minus 2.5-5"
```{r warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
ref_contr <- contrast(emm, method = list(">5 minus 2.5-5" = c(0, -1, 1)), adjust = "none")

res <- summary(ref_contr, infer = TRUE)

res_clean <- as.data.frame(res) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    Year = year_f,
    Contrast = contrast,
    Estimate = estimate,
    Lower.CL = lower.CL,
    Upper.CL = upper.CL,
    P.value = p.value
  )

res_clean <- res_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Estimate = ifelse(is.na(Estimate), "nonEst", sprintf("%.3f", Estimate)),
    Lower.CL = ifelse(is.na(Lower.CL), "NA", sprintf("%.3f", Lower.CL)),
    Upper.CL = ifelse(is.na(Upper.CL), "NA", sprintf("%.3f", Upper.CL)),
    P.value  = ifelse(
                 is.na(P.value), "NA",
                 ifelse(P.value < 0.0001, "<0.0001", sprintf("%.4f", P.value))
               )
  )

res_clean %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Adjusted Difference (>5% minus 2.5–5%) in PLT:RBC Ratio by Year",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = "lcccccc"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```

- The adjusted difference in PLT:RBC ratio between hospitals with >5% and 2.5–5% LTWOB utilization varied 
substantially over time (significant interaction). A marked elevation in PLT:RBC ratio was observed 
among >5% hospitals in 2020, with adjusted differences exceeding 3 units compared with the 2.5–5% group (p < 0.0001).
- A pronounced elevation in the >5% group was observed in 2020 (difference, 3.06; p < 0.0001), representing a strong 
early divergence in component use.
- By 2022, this pattern reversed, with the >5% group demonstrating significantly lower adjusted ratios 
(difference, −1.47; p = 0.003).
- No significant differences were observed in 2019 or 2021, nor in 2023–2025, indicating a return to alignment 
across LTWOB categories in later years.
- For all subsequent years (2023–2025), the categories showed no meaningful differences, with point estimates clustered near zero.
> Temporal fluctuations in PLT:RBC ratio among hospitals with higher LTWOB usage suggest early variability in transfusion 
practices during whole‑blood program rollout, followed by stabilization and convergence across hospitals in subsequent years.
